name: Clara Siagian
affiliation: Bartlett Development Planning Unit, University College London
tagline: People, places, and politics — how young people navigate change, crisis, and the long horizon of Southeast Asia.
bio:
I examine the intersection of people, place, and politics to understand how young people navigate state interventions in the Asia-Pacific. With a focus on Indonesia, my research spans housing, urban development, and climate governance, grounded in ten years of participatory fieldwork.

I have an exceptional track record of independent leadership, including AU$1.1M in funding and six peer-reviewed articles (5 in Q1; 210+ citations). My work translates complex social dynamics into actionable policy knowledge, currently supported by a prestigious postdoctoral appointment at the Bartlett Development Planning Unit, University College London.

Complementing my academic output, I have consulted for the World Bank and UN agencies, bringing research-based policy insights into global development agenda.
methods: Long-term ethnographic fieldwork; longitudinal qualitative panels; oral history; participatory mapping with young people; and policy-engaged research with civil society and government partners.
